title: Reject duplicate MCP-submitted requirements
summary: Feedback #42 requires AutoPlan to identify and reject duplicate requirements sent through MCP. The current MCP create_requirement and create_feedback tools delegate to the shared IntakeService create paths, which always insert new rows. The plan is to add shared duplicate-intake detection before persistence, expose a clear MCP error response that points to the existing record, and cover the behavior with regression tests while preserving normal unique requirement and feedback creation.

tasks.title: Add shared duplicate intake detection before insert
acceptance: createRequirement rejects a new requirement when the same project already has a non-closed requirement with equivalent normalized title and body.
createFeedback rejects a new feedback item when the same project and requirement association already has a non-closed feedback item with equivalent normalized title and body.
Duplicate comparison ignores insignificant whitespace differences such as leading/trailing spaces, repeated spaces, and CRLF versus LF line endings, but does not collapse genuinely different text.
Closed duplicate records do not block a new submission, so users can intentionally re-open previously closed work by creating a fresh intake.
When a duplicate is rejected, no new intake row, attachment row, creation event, or autoRun loop start is produced.

tasks.title: Return a clear MCP duplicate rejection response
acceptance: MCP create_requirement returns isError true for duplicate input and the structured error includes a stable duplicate code plus the existing requirement id.
MCP create_feedback returns isError true for duplicate input and the structured error includes a stable duplicate code plus the existing feedback id.
The duplicate rejection message is human-readable and tells the caller that the requirement or feedback already exists instead of returning a generic validation failure.
Successful unique create_requirement and create_feedback responses keep the existing structuredContent shape, including intake summary, openable link, and snapshot summary.
MCP tool descriptions or generated docs make it clear that duplicate create requests are rejected.

tasks.title: Cover duplicate MCP submissions with regression tests
acceptance: Tests cover duplicate requirement rejection through the shared service path, including whitespace-normalized body and title comparison.
Tests cover duplicate feedback rejection through the shared service path, including requirementId-aware matching.
Tests verify rejected duplicates do not save attachments, add creation events, or start the project loop.
MCP tests verify duplicate create_requirement and create_feedback calls return isError true with the expected duplicate code and existing record id.
Tests verify non-duplicates and closed historical records can still be created normally.

criteria: Static checks pass with no syntax or TypeScript errors.
MCP create_requirement and create_feedback reject duplicate non-closed records in the same project before any persistence side effects occur.
MCP duplicate rejection responses are structured, human-readable, and include enough existing-record context for callers to avoid retry loops.
Unique intake creation, closed-record re-submission, attachments, events, snapshots, and autoRun behavior remain compatible with existing flows.
The rendered AutoPlan Markdown keeps a `## 任务拆解` section with continuous single-line checkbox task rows and a final validation task generated from finalValidation.
